channel.title: NPR Topics: News
channel.description: NPR news, audio, and podcasts. Coverage of breaking stories, national and world news, politics, business, science, technology, and extended coverage of major national and world events.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: It's one of the world's most isolated islands. Here come the bulldozers
item.description: The Indian government is spending $9 billion to create a megaport, airport and city on this remote island. Critics fear the impact on pristine forests and the lives of indigenous inhabitants.
item.content:encoded:
The Indian government is spending $9 billion to create a megaport, airport and city on this remote island. Critics fear the impact on pristine forests and the lives of indigenous inhabitants.

(Image credit: Omkar Khandekar/NPR)
item.dc:creator: Omkar Khandekar

item.title: 1 million people flood Madrid streets to see the pope's flower-carpeted procession
item.description: The crowd cheered and shouted "This is the youth of the pope!" as Pope Leo arrived for Mass at a central Madrid plaza. It's the first papal visit to Spain in 15 years.
item.content:encoded:
The crowd cheered and shouted "This is the youth of the pope!" as Pope Leo arrived for Mass at a central Madrid plaza. It's the first papal visit to Spain in 15 years.

(Image credit: Manu Fernandez)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: At least 12 people shot at an Ohio festival and a search for suspects is still ongoing, police say
item.description: Gunfire erupted Saturday near a busy street festival in Ohio, wounding at least 12 people and sending some eventgoers scrambling for cover while others rushed to help the victims.
item.content:encoded:
Gunfire erupted Saturday near a busy street festival in Ohio, wounding at least 12 people and sending some eventgoers scrambling for cover while others rushed to help the victims.

(Image credit: Paul Sancya)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: 4 takeaways from the U.S. men's final tune-up games before the World Cup
item.description: The U.S. men's national team chose to play a pair of highly-ranked, super competitive teams in the final lead-up to the World Cup: Senegal and Germany. The matches showed the U.S. is ready.
item.content:encoded:
The U.S. men's national team chose to play a pair of highly-ranked, super competitive teams in the final lead-up to the World Cup: Senegal and Germany. The matches showed the U.S. is ready.

(Image credit: Jamie Squire)
item.dc:creator: Becky Sullivan

item.title: When U.S. foreign aid changed, AIDS workers in Africa felt it
item.description: In South Africa and Mozambique, health care providers say cancellation or redirection of U.S. PEPFAR funding under the Trump administration have already endangered vulnerable people and cost lives.
item.content:encoded:
In South Africa and Mozambique, health care providers say cancellation or redirection of U.S. PEPFAR funding under the Trump administration have already endangered vulnerable people and cost lives.

(Image credit: Gulshan Khan for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Juana Summers

item.title: Bumblebees have tiny brains but they can solve problems like chimps and elephants
item.description: New research suggests the fuzzy insects may be capable of spontaneously solving problems the way animals with much larger brains do.
item.content:encoded:
New research suggests the fuzzy insects may be capable of spontaneously solving problems the way animals with much larger brains do.

(Image credit: Mikko Törmänen/University of Oulu)
item.dc:creator: Ari Daniel

item.title: SoFi Stadium workers vote to authorize strike ahead of World Cup
item.description: Negotiations between the union representing the workers, the hospitality group at the Los Angeles stadium and FIFA are set to continue Monday.
item.content:encoded:
Negotiations between the union representing the workers, the hospitality group at the Los Angeles stadium and FIFA are set to continue Monday.

(Image credit: Jae C. Hong)
item.dc:creator: Chandelis Duster

item.title: Why one historian uses social media to remember D-Day in real time
item.description: At the National World War II Memorial, historian Alex Kershaw has found an unlikely way to keep D-Day alive: live social media posts timed to the events of June 6, 1944.
item.content:encoded: At the National World War II Memorial, historian Alex Kershaw has found an unlikely way to keep D-Day alive: live social media posts timed to the events of June 6, 1944.
item.dc:creator: Henry Larson

item.title: Who will face Karen Bass? LA voters still waiting to find out
item.description: Results are still coming in from the mayoral primary in Los Angeles. LAist reporter Frank Stoltze discusses who may emerge to face Karen Bass in November.
item.content:encoded: Results are still coming in from the mayoral primary in Los Angeles. LAist reporter Frank Stoltze discusses who may emerge to face Karen Bass in November.
item.dc:creator: Adrian Florido

item.title: Israel has reportedly used white phosphorus near Lebanese cities and towns. What is it?
item.description: White phosphorus is not banned under international law, but can "create cruel injuries" and indiscriminate harm in civilian areas.
item.content:encoded:
White phosphorus is not banned under international law, but can "create cruel injuries" and indiscriminate harm in civilian areas.

(Image credit: Oliver Laban-Mattei)
item.dc:creator: Stephan Bisaha
